name: Network Forensics
overview:
Network forensics is the capture, recording, and analysis of network traffic to discover the source of security attacks or other incidents. In this learning path, you'll progress from basic packet capture analysis to advanced protocol dissection and attack reconstruction.

You'll work with real-world scenarios from HackTheBox Sherlocks and Challenges, building practical skills that translate directly to SOC analyst and incident responder roles.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of TCP/IP networking (OSI model, common protocols)

coreTools.name: Wireshark
coreTools.description: GUI-based packet analyzer — the gold standard for interactive PCAP analysis. Includes tshark (CLI counterpart) for scripting and automation

coreTools.name: NetworkMiner
coreTools.description: Network forensic analysis tool for extracting files, images, and credentials from PCAP files

coreTools.name: Zeek (Bro)
coreTools.description: Network security monitor that generates high-level logs from packet captures

coreTools.name: hashcat
coreTools.description: GPU-accelerated password cracking — used to crack NTLM hashes, Kerberos tickets, and other credentials extracted from captures

entryRecommendation: Start with **Origins** if you're new to PCAP analysis — it covers FTP traffic in plaintext, so you can focus on learning Wireshark without protocol complexity. If you already know the basics, skip ahead to **Noxious** to dive into Active Directory attacks and NTLM hash extraction.
